system: 4c Si-CHA + CO2 (TraPPE, charges, Ewald, 14A), fresh native re-run this session

kups_tail_test.finding: kUPS DOES apply an LJ tail to the Widom insertion (tail-off drops K_H -16%, q_st -0.49). The 'no tail in Widom path' hypothesis is FALSIFIED. kUPS's tail (+16.3%) is ~4% stronger than native's (+11.9%).

residual_character: q_st-invariant (Δq_st ≤0.3 kJ/mol on matched convention) -> normalization-flavoured (volume / insertion-count / <W> scale). 4c-specific, larger than 6c (-2.4%). Contributors: Ewald convention, CO2 orientational sampling, tail-implementation difference (kUPS +16.3% vs native +11.9%), K_H normalization.
conclusion: kUPS uses TRUNCATED LJ and DOES apply a tail (confirmed). On matched convention (truncated+tail) kUPS is -8.1% vs native (or -11.7% with tail also off vs native). The +2.2% vs the shifted lock is +11.6% shift combined with the -8.1% residual. The residual is q_st-invariant -> a normalization-flavoured scale, 4c-specific and larger than 6c's -2.4%; NOT the +2.8% a no-tail hypothesis would give (that hypothesis was tested and falsified).
